rationale.policy: Each limit is a hard raw-byte/entry ceiling enforced by viteBudgetPlugin during the production build (Rollup generateBundle) and the Workbox precache manifest transform. One byte or one entry over any limit fails the build. Baselines are the current measured actuals plus a small (~4-5%) headroom so routine edits do not flap CI while a material regression is caught.
rationale.initialEntryBytes: Raw bytes of the synchronously-loaded entry chunk (index-*.js). The web-architecture plan's original target was 423707, baselined against a smaller plan-time entry; tasks 1-9 grew the eager entry (pure state cores + consolidated grammar scanner are imported at startup) to ~440910 raw (measured 2026-07-16 in generateBundle), ABOVE that 423707 target. Every other budget here matches the plan number exactly (each is actual + ~4-5% headroom); 423707 alone is stale because the real entry outgrew it, so enforcing it literally would make the build permanently red rather than catch regressions. Rebaselined to the current actual + ~4.8% headroom, consistent with the headroom methodology of the other five budgets. See task-10-report.md for the full discrepancy write-up; the 462016 rebaseline was ratified by the user on 2026-07-16 as the permanent limit (no entry-shrink follow-up owed).
rationale.largestAssetBytes: Largest single output file. Currently the sqlite3 wasm binary (~864752). Cap leaves headroom for a minor wasm bump without silently precaching a much larger binary.
rationale.totalOutputBytes: Sum of every emitted output file. Guards against overall bundle bloat regardless of chunk split.
rationale.precacheBytes: Sum of the Workbox precache manifest entry sizes. This is the offline app-shell weight a cold install downloads. Must stay bounded so install stays fast and quota-safe.
rationale.precacheEntries: Number of precache manifest entries. Guards against an explosion of precached files (e.g. a new eagerly-split dependency family).
rationale.mermaidOwnedBytes: Raw bytes of chunks WHOLLY owned by the Mermaid module graph (Rollup module ownership, not output file-name substrings). Mermaid stays fully precached / never-online under this explicit cap; ownership is all-or-nothing per chunk so the cap cannot absorb unrelated code.
